steps.title: Use the contrast to find what the playwrights intended
steps.content: The sentence says the play had "the opposite effect, wearying audiences instead of energizing them." The contrast signal "instead of" reveals what the playwrights hoped for: something that would energize rather than weary. The blank must describe a quality that means energizing or exciting.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and find the match
steps.content:
Consider every option:
  • Choice A — ‘multifaceted’: Means having many aspects or dimensions; this says nothing about energy or excitement and would not contrast logically with "wearying." ✗
  • Choice B — ‘realistic’: Means true to life; a realistic play could still weary audiences, so this is not the opposite of wearying. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘rousing’: Means exciting, stirring, or energizing—the direct opposite of wearying an audience. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘subtle’: Means understated or delicate; a subtle play is even more likely to weary than energize, not the opposite. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
